holdings.infoBox: IYW is an equity ETF with a total of 144 individual holdings. The top holdings are NVIDIA stock at 14.73%, Apple at 13.16%, Alphabet at 6.57%, Alphabet at 5.33%, and Micron Technology at 5.32%.

overview.description: The iShares U.S. Technology ETF (IYW) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in information technology equity. The fund tracks a market-cap weighted index of US technology companies. IYW was launched on May 15, 2000 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Tips for investing in tech now
data.text: Big tech has dominated the markets, but with tariffs and market uncertainty, what should investors do with the sector now? Ivana Delevska, Spear Invest Founder and CIO, joins Wealth to give some tips ...

data.title: Move on QQQ ETF: XLT and IYM are firing on all cylinders
data.text: Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) has become the biggest technology ETFs with over $286 billion in assets. It has done this well by tracking Nasdaq 100, the most popular tech-focused Nasdaq 100 index in the world...

data.title: Industrials over tech? Gauging productivity play among AI ETFs
data.text: Exchange-traded funds tied to the AI boom might be outperforming this year, but momentum is shifting as investors weigh the tech-fueled hype and higher rates.

data.title: Tech ETFs get a boost as Apple becomes first company in the world worth $2 trillion
data.text: Exchange-traded funds with big bets on Apple Inc. AAPL, +0.92% bounced Wednesday when the tech giant added 1.1% and became the first company to cross $2 trillion in market capitalization.
